data.title: Liquidia: Strong Yutrepia Uptake and Sustained Profitability Support Buy Rating
data.description:
Gaurav Maini, an analyst from LifeSci Capital, has initiated a new Buy rating on Liquidia Technologies (LQDA).

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Gaurav Maini has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors that highlight Liquidia’s strong commercial momentum and improving fundamentals. He points to the rapid uptake of Yutrepia in both pulmonary arterial hypertension and PH-ILD, noting that prescription growth has been robust despite the product only launching in mid-2025, and that revenue has already scaled to a meaningful level.

Maini also emphasizes that Liquidia has reached profitability for a second consecutive quarter, supported by Yutrepia’s performance and a solid cash position that can fund continued investment in new programs. He views the balanced adoption across PAH and PH-ILD, the growing willingness of physicians to switch patients from existing prostacyclin therapies, and the planned exploratory studies as evidence of substantial remaining market opportunity and a durable growth trajectory.

In another report released today, Needham also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $54.00 price target.

data.title: Downgrade to Hold on Enhabit’s Takeout by Kinderhook as Deal Caps Upside and Shifts Focus to Transaction Dynamics
data.description:
In a report released today, Ryan Langston from TD Cowen downgraded Enhabit, Inc to a Hold, with a price target of $13.80.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Ryan Langston has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors tied primarily to the announced acquisition. He shifted the recommendation from Buy to Hold after Enhabit agreed to be purchased by private equity firm Kinderhook in an all-cash deal at $13.80 per share, and he set his price target equal to that offer, implying limited upside from current levels.

While he continues to project stable 2026 and 2027 adjusted EBITDA of $106 million and $116 million, respectively, he views the transaction valuation of roughly 9.6x 2027 EBITDA-NCI as appropriately capturing the company’s prospects. Recent quarterly results were generally in line with expectations, but with no forward guidance and a high likelihood the deal closes as announced, he sees the stock now trading primarily on deal dynamics rather than fundamental re-rating potential.

In another report released today, TipRanks – OpenAI also reiterated a Hold rating on the stock with a $14.50 price target.

data.title: Buy Rating Backed by Temporary Q4 Disruption, Long-Term Government Contracts, and NexoBrid-Driven Growth
data.description:
In a report released today, Josh Jennings from TD Cowen reiterated a Buy rating on Mediwound, with a price target of $25.00.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Josh Jennings has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to both short‑term disruptions and long‑term growth drivers. He views the weak fourth‑quarter results as largely the product of a temporary U.S. government shutdown that delayed contract activity, while noting that the company still exited the year with a solid cash position and better‑than‑expected bottom‑line results.

He also highlights reaffirmed multi‑year revenue guidance that points to strong growth through 2028, underpinned by ongoing BARDA and Department of Defense contracts and the scaling of NexoBrid. With a major manufacturing expansion set to multiply NexoBrid capacity and support margin improvement, alongside the anticipated launch contribution from EscharEx later in the decade, Jennings sees a compelling path for revenue acceleration and profitability, supporting his Buy recommendation.

According to TipRanks, Jennings is a 4-star analyst with an average return of 4.3% and a 49.10% success rate. Jennings covers the Healthcare sector, focusing on stocks such as Boston Scientific, TransMedics Group, and Abbott Laboratories.

In another report released yesterday, Craig-Hallum also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $0.00 price target.

data.title: GH Research: Phase 3 Readiness, Expanding Indications, and Valuation Upside Reinforce Buy Rating
data.description:
Analyst Ami Fadia from Needham maintained a Buy rating on GH Research and increased the price target to $32.00 from $19.00.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Ami Fadia has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including GH Research’s slightly better‑than‑expected 4Q25 loss per share, which reflected non‑operating items while core spending stayed in line with forecasts. She also emphasizes that the company’s pivotal Phase 3 trial in treatment‑resistant depression remains on schedule for a 2026 start, following recent FDA clearance of its IND filing.

In addition, Fadia highlights the ongoing Phase 1 pharmacokinetic bridging study using GH Research’s own delivery device, a critical prerequisite for the Phase 3 program and eventual commercialization strategy. She further notes early but encouraging signals from GH‑001 in bipolar II disorder and postpartum depression, and modest model updates that support raising her price target to $32, reinforcing the Buy recommendation.

In another report released on March 2, H.C. Wainwright also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $70.00 price target.

data.title: BJ’s Wholesale Club: Near-Term Margin Pressures Masking Long-Term Upside and Undervalued Growth Potential
data.description:
William Blair analyst Phillip Blee has maintained their bullish stance on BJ stock, giving a Buy rating today.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Phillip Blee has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to BJ’s near- and long‑term outlook. While he acknowledges that early‑year earnings will be pressured by weaker margins, higher investments, and weather‑related softness in comparable sales, he views these issues as temporary and largely reflected in current trading levels.

He expects performance to improve meaningfully from late spring as comparisons ease, seasonal categories recover margins, and new stores and distribution investments begin to contribute. In his view, the current valuation, at under 20 times his 2027 EPS estimate, does not fully reflect potential upside from accelerating comps, margin recovery, and continued digital momentum that strengthens BJ’s competitive position versus peers.

In another report released today, TipRanks – OpenAI also reiterated a Buy rating on the stock with a $113.00 price target.

data.title: Early Komzifti Traction and Clinical Catalysts Underpin Buy Rating and 2028 Growth Outlook

Phil Nadeau has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, primarily the early commercial traction of Komzifti and its favorable reimbursement setup. Initial quarterly sales, while modest, align with expectations for a launch phase, and the rapid achievement of coverage with roughly four-fifths of commercial insurers suggests a supportive access environment that should facilitate continued uptake.

data.title: Brilliant Earth: Mixed Quarter, Margin Pressure, and Valuation Risks Justify Hold Amid Long-Term Jewelry Growth Prospects
data.description:
William Blair analyst Dylan Carden has maintained their neutral stance on BRLT stock, giving a Hold rating today.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Dylan Carden has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors related to both near-term headwinds and longer-term opportunities. Brilliant Earth’s latest quarter showed only mixed performance, and management’s outlook for the first quarter points to pressured profitability as rising metal costs push margins into negative midsingle digits, leaving the year heavily dependent on a stronger fourth quarter.

At the same time, he notes encouraging signs such as improving demand trends, growth in average selling prices, and expanding contribution from the fine-jewelry segment, which could become a key driver of repeat purchases. However, the current valuation already discounts a slow and uncertain recovery in sales and earnings, while elevated commodity costs and limited pricing flexibility in fine jewelry pose ongoing risks, leading him to wait for clearer progress toward the company’s original growth and margin targets before turning more positive on the stock.

In another report released today, Telsey Advisory also maintained a Hold rating on the stock with a $2.00 price target.

data.title: Burlington Stores: Accelerating Sales, Strategic Investments, and Attractive Valuation Support Buy Rating
data.description:
William Blair analyst Dylan Carden has maintained their bullish stance on BURL stock, giving a Buy rating today.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Dylan Carden has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Burlington’s accelerating sales trends and improving setup into 2026. He highlights that strong holiday results, easier comparisons, and plans to rebuild inventory in tariff-affected categories support sustained low- to mid-single-digit comparable sales, especially as consumers remain resilient and may see a lift from larger tax refunds.

Carden also notes that recent merchandising and localization investments, along with new distribution capacity, should enhance consistency in performance even though near-term margin pressures from tariffs and distribution ramp-up may weigh on profitability. He argues that the current valuation, at about 25 times his 2027 EPS estimate and below key peers like TJX and Ross, leaves room for multiple expansion if Burlington can deliver steadier comps, making the risk/reward attractive at present levels.

According to TipRanks, Carden is a 4-star analyst with an average return of 8.6% and a 52.41% success rate. Carden covers the Consumer Cyclical sector, focusing on stocks such as Savers Value Village Inc., On Holding AG, and thredUP.
